articles.title: INVESTORS PREDICT AI IS COMING FOR LABOR IN 2026
articles.summary: AI could automate 11.7% of jobs, prompting employers to reduce entry-level positions and justify layoffs. Enterprise VCs foresee significant AI impact on the workforce in 2026, with companies shifting budgets from labor to AI investments. While AI proponents argue it enhances productivity, concerns persist about job automation leading to increased unemployment.

articles.title: ELON MUSK ENVISIONS HUMANOID ROBOTS EVERYWHERE. CHINA MAY BE THE FIRST TO MAKE IT A REALITY
articles.summary: Elon Musk highlights humanoid robots for Tesla's future, but Chinese companies may lead with mass production in 2026. China focuses on robotics to tackle demographic and economic challenges, employing its manufacturing strengths despite chip restrictions. Analysts expect China's robot market to surpass the US initially, though challenges like AI limitations and high costs persist.

articles.title: THE MEMORY WARS: WHY AI'S FUTURE DEPENDS ON 16-HI HBM
articles.summary: AI is hitting a memory bottleneck: massive models need terabytes of fast memory. NVIDIA's 16-Hi HBM orders, 3D-stacked SRAM, and Groq deal aim to fix this, enabling near-instant inference and huge model scales. The result: AI can finally reach its potential in chat, robotics, and scientific discovery, shifting the limit from hardware to imagination.

articles.title: HOW AI IS RESHAPING ENTRY-LEVEL TECH JOBS
articles.summary: AI reshapes entry-level tech roles by automating routine tasks, leading to a 25% decline in hiring at major tech firms. Despite a drop in programmer jobs, positions like information security analysts and AI engineers are growing. Education must adapt, emphasizing AI proficiency and experiential learning, while apprenticeship models offer practical experience and bridge skill gaps.

articles.title: END-TO-END TEST-TIME TRAINING
articles.summary: E2E is a long-context language modeling approach that reframes the task as continual learning. Using standard Transformers with sliding-window attention, the model learns at test time via next-token prediction and meta-learns during training for better initialization.

articles.title: FINANCIAL KNOWLEDGE IN LLMS
articles.summary: FinCDM proposes a cognitive diagnosis framework for evaluating financial LLMs at the skill level, moving beyond single-score benchmarks.

articles.title: LMCACHE
articles.summary: LMCache is an open-source KV-cache acceleration layer for LLM serving that stores and reuses transformer key‑value cache chunks (across GPU, CPU, disk, and Redis), enabling 3–10× faster response times and significantly reduced GPU compute under long-context and multi-turn scenarios.

articles.title: THE US ARMY IS PREPARING TO TRAIN ITS FIRST AI SPECIALISTS
articles.summary: The US Army will start training AI/ML officer specialists through its Volunteer Transfer Incentive Program beginning in January. Training will focus on building, deploying, and maintaining AI systems, leveraging commercial AI solutions. This initiative aims to develop in-house expertise for efficient AI integration across warfighting functions.

articles.title: HARVARD'S CS 249R
articles.summary: An open-source textbook for Harvard's CS249R course on deep learning and reinforcement learning with practical examples and lectures.

articles.title: 13 TIMES AI ACTUALLY DELIVERED
articles.summary: AI in marketing helps streamline tasks like image generation, ad optimization, and content creation without replacing human creativity and strategy. Brands like Heads or Tails Pup and Very Ireland saw significant sales and engagement boosts by leveraging AI for design and title optimization, while maintaining human oversight. AI has proven valuable for fast, cost-effective content production, yet relying solely on its outputs can mislead consumers, highlighting the need for strategic human intervention.

articles.title: SAM ALTMAN TACKLES DANGERS OF AI WITH NEW ROLE
articles.summary: Sam Altman is directly hiring a Head of Preparedness at OpenAI to manage AI threats, including cyber misuse and biological risks.

articles.title: THE $130 BILLION COMEBACK: WHY APPLE'S "SLOW" AI STRATEGY IS A 2026 TRAP
articles.summary: Apple's "slow" AI strategy hinges on $130 billion in cash reserves, focusing on user experience rather than competing in the AI arms race.

articles.title: WHY AI STARTUPS MUST RELEARN GROWTH EVERY QUARTER
articles.summary: Product-market fit resets every three months at Lovable because AI capabilities and user expectations shift too fast to rely on long-term scaling, even past $200M ARR.
